methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

transformations: Raw quote bytes parsed into V3Struct.ParsedV3QuoteStruct
PEM cert chain split and Base64 decoded into DER
DER decoded into ECSha256Certificate structs
Signature bytes extracted and verified via ES256/RSA
sha256 hash of quote computed for return data
data_flows.trust_changes: Untrusted external data goes through parsing and cryptographic verification to become trusted attestation result
validation_points: Header version, attestation key type, tee type, vendor ID checks
Certificate chain verification including expiry and revocation
ECDSA signature verification on enclave reports
exposure_risks: verifyParsedQuote is external and accepts pre-parsed struct - bypasses raw quote parsing validation
parseCerificationChainBytes calls pemCertLib via staticcall on user-provided address in parseInput
manipulation_risks: If owner sets permissive TCB info or QE identity, invalid attestations could pass
Owner can toggle _checkLocalEnclaveReport to bypass MRENCLAVE/MRSIGNER checks
Owner can remove revoked serial numbers to accept revoked certificates

path: Conditionally sync L1 state root to SignalService
transformations: publicInputHash computed from blockhashes ring buffer
basefee computed from gasExcess + parentGasUsed via EIP-1559 bonding curve
gasExcess adjusted by L1 block issuance
data_flows.trust_changes: GOLDEN_TOUCH_ADDRESS is trusted system address providing L1 data to L2
exposure_risks: If GOLDEN_TOUCH_ADDRESS is compromised, arbitrary L1 data can be anchored
manipulation_risks: L1 block sync only happens when _l1BlockId > lastSyncedBlock + BLOCK_SYNC_THRESHOLD, allowing gaps in state root syncing

transformations: ABI decode _data into (uint64 txId, bytes txdata)
txdata executed as call to self
sinks: address(this) via low-level call with arbitrary txdata
data_flows.trust_changes: Bridge-delivered message from owner on remote chain gets executed as local self-call
exposure_risks: The self-call with arbitrary txdata allows calling any public/external function on the contract
Not guarded by nonReentrant - explicitly noted in code comment
manipulation_risks: If bridge contract is compromised, arbitrary txdata can be executed
Sequential txId prevents replay but allows DoS if a txId is skipped

data_flows.source: Merkle proof and claim data from users
transformations: User+amount encoded with prefix string and hashed
Hash verified against merkle root with proof
sinks: Token transfers from vault to user
data_flows.trust_changes: Untrusted user proof verified against trusted merkle root set by owner
validation_points: ongoingClaim modifier checks merkleRoot, claimStart, claimEnd
exposure_risks: ERC20Airdrop.claimAndDelegate: delegationData can be crafted by anyone calling the function
manipulation_risks: Owner can change merkleRoot via setConfig at any time, potentially invalidating pending claims or enabling new ones
ERC20Airdrop2: claimed amounts are stored per-user but claim hash is based on (user, amount) - a user cannot claim different amounts

data_flows.source: Grant parameters from owner, withdrawal requests from recipients
path: getMyGrantSummary computes amountOwned, amountUnlocked, amountToWithdraw, costToWithdraw
Transfer taikoToken from sharedVault to recipient
Transfer costToken from recipient to sharedVault
transformations: _calcAmount: linear vesting based on block.timestamp relative to start/cliff/period
costToWithdraw = (amountUnlocked / 1e18) * costPerToken - costPaid
amountToWithdraw = amountUnlocked - amountWithdrawn
data_flows.trust_changes: Owner sets grant parameters; recipient can only withdraw according to vesting schedule
exposure_risks: taikoToken.transferFrom (not safeTransferFrom) used for token transfer from vault
manipulation_risks: Precision loss in costToWithdraw calculation due to division before multiplication
withdraw(address, bytes) allows anyone to trigger withdrawal with valid signature

path: Extract serial number, validity dates, public key, signature
For PCK certs: extract SGX extensions (FMSPC, PCEID, TCB SVNs)
_findTcb extracts CPU SVN values and PCESVN
transformations: DER bytes parsed into ASN1 tree structure
Date strings converted to timestamps via X509DateUtils
Public key and signature bytes trimmed to expected lengths
SGX extension OIDs matched and values extracted
SVN byte values converted to uint16 with endianness handling
data_flows.trust_changes: Raw DER bytes are parsed without cryptographic validation at this stage - validation happens later in _verifyCertChain
exposure_risks: Malformed DER could cause out-of-bounds in ASN1 parsing
manipulation_risks: The _findTcb function has an off-by-one risk: iterates SGX_TCB_CPUSVN_SIZE+1 times but cpusvns array is only SGX_TCB_CPUSVN_SIZE, so when PCESVN OID is not the last element, array index can overflow
notBefore tag validation has a logic bug: (notBeforeTag != 0x17 && notBeforeTag == 0x18) always passes for 0x18 tags incorrectly

transformations: Gas excess accumulation with parent gas used
Gas issuance subtraction based on L1 block count
Exponential function for base fee calculation
Clamped to uint64 max
data_flows.trust_changes: System-level calculation, inputs from trusted anchor transaction
manipulation_risks: If gasExcess is 0, base fee is always 1 regardless of congestion
Large gap between lastSyncedBlock and _l1BlockId could cause large issuance subtraction resetting excess to 1

core_rules: SGX attestation must pass full verification chain: quote parsing, enclave identity, TCB check, cert chain, signature verification
TaikoL2 anchor must be called by GOLDEN_TOUCH_ADDRESS and maintains a ring buffer of block hashes for public input integrity
Cross-chain ownership requires sequential transaction IDs and bridge-verified source chain + owner
Merkle airdrop claims are one-time per leaf hash and bounded by time window
TimelockTokenPool enforces vesting (grant) and unlock schedules independently with linear interpolation
Certificate chain trust is anchored to ROOTCA_PUBKEY_HASH constant
economic_incentives: EIP-1559 base fee adjusts based on gas usage vs target, incentivizing optimal gas usage
TimelockTokenPool costPerToken creates a purchase requirement for token vesting
ERC20Airdrop2 linear withdrawal window prevents immediate dump of airdropped tokens
governance_mechanisms: AutomataDcapV3Attestation owner can configure trusted enclaves, TCB info, QE identity, and revocation lists
TaikoL2EIP1559Configurable owner can change EIP-1559 parameters
MerkleClaimable owner can update merkle root, claim start, and claim end at any time
CrossChainOwned allows remote chain owner to execute arbitrary functions via bridge

edge_cases.scenario: PEMCertChainLib.decodeCert notBefore tag validation has logic error
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should reject certificates where notBefore tag is neither 0x17 (UTCTime) nor 0x18 (GeneralizedTime)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: The condition (notBeforeTag != 0x17 && notBeforeTag == 0x18) is equivalent to (notBeforeTag == 0x18), meaning it ONLY rejects 0x18 tags (GeneralizedTime) while accepting any other non-0x17 tag. This is inverted from intended behavior.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A crafted certificate with an invalid notBefore tag type (e.g., 0x19) would pass validation, potentially allowing certificates with incorrectly parsed validity dates to be accepted as valid. The timestamp parsing would produce garbage values.

edge_cases.scenario: PEMCertChainLib._findTcb cpusvns array index overflow when PCESVN is not the last SVN entry
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should correctly populate cpusvns[0..15] with CPU SVN values and separately store PCESVN
edge_cases.actual_behavior: The loop iterates SGX_TCB_CPUSVN_SIZE+1 (17) times. The array cpusvns has size 16. If PCESVN OID is matched, pcesvn is set but the else branch is skipped. However, if a non-PCESVN entry appears at index 16, it would write to cpusvns[16] which is out of bounds. In Solidity 0.8.x this would revert.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If the PCK certificate has SVN entries in unexpected order (PCESVN not at the end), or has unexpected extra entries, the function could revert, causing denial of service for attestation verification.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Cost should be proportional to tokens unlocked with full precision
edge_cases.actual_behavior: costToWithdraw = (amountUnlocked / 1e18) * costPerToken - costPaid. Division by 1e18 before multiplication truncates, losing up to (1e18 - 1) units per calculation. For tokens with non-zero costPerToken, this means the recipient pays slightly less cost than intended.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: By withdrawing in small increments, a recipient could accumulate precision loss benefits. Each withdrawal loses up to costPerToken * (1e18 - 1) / 1e18 in cost. With many small withdrawals this could be meaningful, but the grant is one-time per recipient limiting exploitation.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Linear vesting should compute _amount * elapsed / _period precisely
edge_cases.actual_behavior: _amount * uint64(block.timestamp - _start) / _period — the uint64 cast of (block.timestamp - _start) truncates if the time difference exceeds uint64 max, but this is ~584 billion years so practically safe. However, _amount is uint128 and _period is uint32, so _amount * uint64(elapsed) can overflow uint128 for large amounts and long periods.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: For very large grant amounts (close to uint128 max) combined with large elapsed times, the multiplication could overflow. However, grant amounts are validated as uint128 and practical elapsed time is bounded.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Linear unlock from claimEnd to claimEnd + withdrawalWindow
edge_cases.actual_behavior: getBalance computes timeBasedAllowance = balance * (min(timestamp, claimEnd + withdrawalWindow) - claimEnd) / withdrawalWindow. If withdrawalWindow is 0, this causes division by zero revert.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If initialized with withdrawalWindow = 0, no user can ever withdraw. This is an initialization configuration issue.

edge_cases.scenario: AutomataDcapV3Attestation._checkTcbLevels returns (true, TCB_UNRECOGNIZED) when no TCB level matches
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should indicate failure or unrecognized status clearly
edge_cases.actual_behavior: When no TCB level matches the PCK's SVNs, it returns (true, TCB_UNRECOGNIZED). The TCB_UNRECOGNIZED status is then checked by _attestationTcbIsValid which returns false for it. So the final result is correct, but the intermediate 'true' is misleading and could be a source of bugs if _checkTcbLevels result is used differently in the future.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No direct exploitation currently since _attestationTcbIsValid correctly rejects TCB_UNRECOGNIZED, but a future code change relying on the bool return could introduce a vulnerability.

edge_cases.scenario: AutomataDcapV3Attestation owner has no transfer mechanism
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Owner should be transferable via 2-step process or similar safety mechanism
edge_cases.actual_behavior: The owner is set in constructor and there is no function to change it. If the owner key is lost, all admin functions become inaccessible permanently.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Not an exploitation risk but an operational risk. No way to update TCB info, revoke certificates, or change trusted enclaves if owner key is compromised or lost.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Both paths should enforce equivalent validation
edge_cases.actual_behavior: verifyParsedQuote(ParsedV3QuoteStruct) accepts pre-parsed input and calls validateParsedInput which checks struct field lengths but NOT the raw quote binary structure. Some validations from parseInput (like localAuthDataSize consistency) are bypassed. The signedQuoteData is reconstructed from struct fields via abi.encodePacked which may not match the original raw quote bytes.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: An attacker could craft a ParsedV3QuoteStruct that passes validateParsedInput checks but where the reconstructed signedQuoteData differs from what was actually signed. The signature verification uses the reconstructed bytes, so if the reconstruction matches the attestation key's signature, it passes. However, the header and enclave report fields must still match. The risk is that abi.encodePacked reconstruction may produce different bytes than the original binary format.

edge_cases.scenario: SigVerifyLib.verifyES256Signature uses assert(success) for external staticcall
edge_cases.actual_behavior: assert(success) will consume all remaining gas on failure and revert with Panic. If ES256VERIFIER contract is not deployed or has a bug, verification always reverts instead of returning false.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If ES256VERIFIER is destroyed or becomes non-functional, all ES256 signature verifications will fail with panic, causing DoS for the entire attestation system.

edge_cases.scenario: TaikoL2 gasExcess = 0 disables dynamic base fee
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Base fee should always reflect network congestion
edge_cases.actual_behavior: When gasExcess is 0, _calc1559BaseFee skips all computation and returns basefee_ = 1. The anchor function adds _parentGasUsed to gasExcess but only if gasExcess > 0. So once gasExcess reaches 0, it stays at 0 permanently.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If gasExcess somehow reaches 0 (through large issuance subtraction), the base fee is permanently stuck at 1, enabling very cheap spam transactions on L2.

edge_cases.scenario: ERC20Airdrop uses transferFrom instead of safeTransferFrom for token transfer
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should safely handle non-standard ERC20 tokens
edge_cases.actual_behavior: IERC20(token).transferFrom(vault, user, amount) does not use SafeERC20, so tokens that don't return bool on transferFrom would cause a revert.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If the airdropped token is non-standard, claims would fail. Since token is set at initialization and is expected to be TaikoToken, risk is low.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should prevent reentrant calls for safety
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Explicitly noted that nonReentrant is NOT used because the function calls address(this).call(txdata) which re-enters the contract. This means the called function could potentially call back into onMessageInvocation if it goes through the bridge.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: The sequential txId check (txId == nextTxId) provides some protection, but if the self-call increments nextTxId (via another bridge message processed within the call), re-entrancy could execute multiple transactions in one call.

edge_cases.scenario: MerkleClaimable setConfig can change merkleRoot mid-claim period
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should not allow changing root during active claims or should have migration period
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Owner can call setConfig at any time to change claimStart, claimEnd, and merkleRoot. This can invalidate ongoing claims or enable previously invalid claims.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A malicious or compromised owner could change the merkle root to include unauthorized recipients or exclude legitimate ones. Already-claimed hashes remain marked, but new root could have different leaves.

assumption_violations: PEMCertChainLib assumes DER encoding follows strict X.509 structure; malformed DER could cause unexpected parsing behavior in ASN1 library
V3Parser assumes quote binary format strictly matches Intel SGX DCAP V3 specification; deviations could cause incorrect parsing
TimelockTokenPool assumes taikoToken and costToken are standard ERC20 with no fee-on-transfer; fee tokens would break accounting
TaikoL2 assumes blockhash() returns correct values for recent blocks; on non-standard EVM chains this might differ
SigVerifyLib assumes ES256VERIFIER always returns without reverting (assert); if it reverts, entire attestation fails
Certificate chain validation assumes exactly 3 certificates (PCK, Intermediate, Root); different chain lengths would fail
ERC20Airdrop2 assumes claimEnd + withdrawalWindow does not overflow uint64
game_theory_risks: AutomataDcapV3Attestation owner is a single point of trust for all attestation configuration - centralization risk
TimelockTokenPool void function allows owner to claw back unvested tokens, creating trust asymmetry between owner and recipients
ERC20Airdrop2 linear unlock can be gamed by claiming right before claimEnd to maximize withdrawal window
TaikoL2EIP1559Configurable owner can manipulate gas pricing by changing config and gasExcess simultaneously
integration_risks: PEMCertChainLib is called via interface cast in V3Parser.parseCerificationChainBytes - if address changes or is wrong, cert parsing fails silently
SignalService integration in TaikoL2.anchor relies on resolve('signal_service') returning correct address
Bridge integration in CrossChainOwned relies on resolve('bridge') and correct IBridge.context() implementation
ES256VERIFIER external contract dependency in SigVerifyLib - if contract is upgraded or destroyed, all ECDSA verification breaks
ERC20Airdrop vault must have sufficient allowance for the airdrop contract to transfer tokens

calculation_errors.description: Line: (notBeforeTag != 0x17 && notBeforeTag == 0x18) — this condition is logically equivalent to (notBeforeTag == 0x18). The intended check should be (notBeforeTag != 0x17 && notBeforeTag != 0x18) to reject invalid tags. Current code rejects GeneralizedTime (0x18) notBefore tags while accepting arbitrary invalid tags.
calculation_errors.impact: Certificates with non-standard date encoding types in notBefore field are incorrectly accepted

calculation_errors.description: uint128 _amountUnlocked = amountUnlocked / 1e18; costToWithdraw = _amountUnlocked * r.grant.costPerToken - r.costPaid; Division before multiplication causes precision loss of up to 1e18-1 tokens worth of cost
calculation_errors.impact: Recipients pay slightly less cost than intended per withdrawal

calculation_errors.description: svnValue = svnValueBytes.length < 2 ? uint16(bytes2(svnValueBytes)) / 256 : uint16(bytes2(svnValueBytes)); For 1-byte values, bytes2() left-pads the byte into the high byte, then divides by 256 to get the value. This works correctly for single bytes but the approach is fragile and assumes specific ASN1 integer encoding.
calculation_errors.impact: Low - works correctly for standard Intel SGX certificate format but fragile to non-standard encodings

calculation_errors.description: isvProdIdPackBE = (enclaveReport.isvProdId >> 8) | (enclaveReport.isvProdId << 8) — for uint16, the left shift by 8 produces correct byte swap. This is correct for uint16 big-endian conversion.

token_semantics.notes: Used in TimelockTokenPool with transferFrom (not safeTransferFrom). Used in ERC20Airdrop with transferFrom. Assumes standard ERC20 behavior. If fee-on-transfer, accounting in TimelockTokenPool breaks.
Used in TimelockTokenPool with safeTransferFrom. USD stablecoin expected. Precision in costToWithdraw depends on 1e18 divisor.
Must support IVotes.delegateBySig for ERC20Airdrop.claimAndDelegate. transferFrom used without SafeERC20 wrapper.
Used with transferFrom in withdraw. If fee-on-transfer, users receive less than withdrawableAmount computed by getBalance.

invariants.name: totalAmountGranted_conservation
invariants.description: totalAmountGranted = sum of all original grant amounts. After voiding, totalAmountGranted unchanged, totalAmountVoided tracks reduced amounts.

invariants.name: withdrawal_bounded
invariants.description: amountWithdrawn <= amountUnlocked at all times for each recipient

invariants.name: public_input_hash_integrity
invariants.description: publicInputHash must equal keccak256 of the 256-element ring buffer containing 255 blockhashes + chainid

invariants.name: claim_uniqueness
invariants.description: Each claim hash can only be claimed once regardless of merkle root changes

invariants.name: gasExcess_nonzero_invariant
invariants.description: If gasExcess starts > 0, after _calc1559BaseFee it should remain >= 1 (excess is set to max(excess-issuance, 1)). However if it starts at 0, it stays at 0.

invariants.name: cert_chain_trust_anchor
invariants.description: All certificate chain verifications must terminate at a certificate whose issuer public key hashes to ROOTCA_PUBKEY_HASH
